> *Your savings are $4,200 short of a three-month reserve and $20,400 short of a six-month reserve.*

You should keep **at least $16,200** in emergency savings and work toward **$32,400**.

| Measure | Amount |
|---|---:|
| Observed monthly spending | $5,400 |
| Current savings | $12,000 |
| Current coverage | 2.2 months |
| 3-month minimum | $16,200 |
| 6-month target | $32,400 |

**Recommendation:** Direct your observed **$900 monthly surplus** into savings. You would reach the three-month minimum in about **5 months** and the six-month target in about **23 months**, assuming cash flow remains steady.

I’d favor the six-month target because childcare is a substantial fixed cost. Your snapshot also doesn’t clearly identify housing or all transportation obligations, so the target may need adjustment once those are confirmed. Keep this reserve in an accessible, insured savings account—not invested in your brokerage account.

*Data is as of July 13, 2026, using complete calendar months April–June; the accounts are marked as needing a fresh sync.*
